requirements.site: fresh MariaDB-backed Frappe v16 demo site
requirements.actor: enabled user with scoped Muster bindings and native Frappe permissions
requirements.approver: different enabled user with Muster Approver authority
requirements.registered_app: administrator-reviewed disposable custom app with an isolated source root
requirements.cleanup: use the native Change Set rollback gate for artifacts and a reviewed reverse patch for app code

scenarios.prompt: Add a required Customer field called Service Region after Customer Group, show it in list view, and let us filter by it.
positive_evidence: visible Customize Form and Customer form
scenarios.negative_probe: request an eval: dependency and verify preview rejects executable metadata
scenarios.reversal: rollback the exact verified Change Set and reread effective Customer meta

scenarios.prompt: Create a custom DocType Muster Demo Site Visit with clean sections for customer, visit date, owner, notes and status, then add a Desk Page and Workspace for service operations.
scenarios.negative_probe: put active HTML in Workspace content and verify compilation fails before effect
scenarios.reversal: rollback Workspace, Page and DocType in dependency-safe reverse order

scenarios.prompt: Build a parameterized Query Report for open site visits and a trusted Script Report for the service SLA summary.
scenarios.negative_probe: supply raw report_script or an unregistered implementation key and verify denial
scenarios.reversal: rollback both Report documents and confirm report routes no longer resolve

scenarios.prompt: Create a safe Jinja print format for Muster Demo Site Visit using its name, customer, visit date and status.
positive_evidence: rendered PDF and Print Format reread
scenarios.negative_probe: request a Jinja statement, method call or script tag and verify preview rejects it
scenarios.reversal: rollback the Print Format and verify it disappears from the print selector

scenarios.prompt: Publish a login-required Web Form for a site visit request and a static Web Page explaining the request process.
scenarios.negative_probe: request an external route, script event handler or dynamic template and verify fail-closed validation
scenarios.reversal: rollback both routes and verify they return not found

scenarios.prompt: Notify the Support Team in Frappe when a new site visit is created and rotate open visits between two support users on weekdays.
positive_evidence: system notification received by an authorized user
scenarios.negative_probe: request a function call in the assignment condition or recipient code and verify rejection
scenarios.reversal: rollback both automation documents and prove a fresh visit produces neither notification nor assignment

scenarios.prompt: In the registered demo app, add a controller validation, hook, patch and Vue service dashboard for Muster Demo Site Visit, with tests and an upgrade-safe migration.
scenarios.negative_probe: attempt an out-of-scope path, symlink escape, dirty-source drift and failing test; every case must leave source and site unchanged
scenarios.reversal: apply a separately reviewed reverse patch and rerun migrate, build and upgrade tests through the fixed deployment registry

global_verification: every native artifact is reread independently after apply
schema or permission epoch drift forces a new preview
idempotent replay returns prior verified evidence without a second effect
rollback refuses to clobber an artifact edited after apply
all prompts remain in Ask Muster; native forms are opened only for visible attended proof
